The Artist

A Decade of
Practice. A World
of Collectors.

Ralph Kerle is one of Australia's most collected fine art photographers, with works held in private and corporate collections across more than twenty countries. His large-format fine art photography — capturing light on water surfaces, coastal and ocean forms, urban reflections and aerial perspectives — occupies the rare space between photography and painting. Every commission is realised from Ralph's extensive fine art library; the work begins with Ralph's creative vision, not a client brief.

Exhibitions in Berlin, Lisbon, Melbourne, Riga, Dubai, Palm Beach (USA) and Vilnius. Featured International Exhibition at the Rothko Museum. Featured in Art & Style Magazine, Photo Review and the Australian Weekend Magazine. Based in Sydney. Available for commissions worldwide.

How It Works

From First Conversation
to Finished Work

01
On-Site Consult

Ralph visits your space in person. He assesses your wall, natural light, existing palette, and how you live — at no cost and no obligation.

02
Concept & Scale

Working from Ralph's fine art library, the right work is identified for your space. Scale, theme, palette and finish are then confirmed — translated from your taste and the character of your space.

03
Digital Mockup

A custom digital rendering shows exactly how the proposed work will look on your wall — scaled, framed, in your actual room — before you commit.

04
Creation in Studio

The artwork is created in Ralph's studio to museum print standards — archival inks, fine art substrates, precision framing. Each piece is drawn from his extensive fine art library and realised as a unique work made only for your space.

05
Delivery & Install

White-glove delivery and professional installation. The work arrives ready to hang, and Ralph ensures it is placed perfectly in your space.

Ready to Transform Your Space?

"Every commission begins in my fine art library — work I have created over years, capturing light and form in ways that cannot be directed or repeated. What you commission is drawn from that body of work and made only for your space. There are no editions, no reprints."

What a Commission Costs
